steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures around the blank
steps.content: The text begins with a dependent prepositional phrase (“In addition to having hard hooves that can dig into nearly any groove or”), immediately followed by an independent clause (“mountain goats have slender bodies ideal for scaling nearly ninety-degree cliffs”).

steps.title: Apply the comma rule for introductory phrases
steps.content: A comma separates an introductory phrase from the main clause. “Crack,” (with a comma) correctly creates this required separation. A and B introduce awkward extra “or” structures, and C omits the required comma. That matches choice D — D is correct.
